summary: Super Admin Upgrades Tab Feature - Full testing complete. All 14 backend tests passed (100%). All frontend features verified working: Upgrades tab with badge count (4), 4 summary cards (Critical=2, Warning=2, Total=4, Revenue=₹3,996/month), admin list with CRITICAL/WARNING badges, progress bars showing usage vs limits, Upgrade modal with plan selection, Overview tab amber alert banner.
passed_tests: GET /api/superadmin/admins-needing-upgrades requires authentication (returns 403)
GET /api/superadmin/admins-needing-upgrades requires superadmin role (returns 403 for regular admin)
GET /api/superadmin/admins-needing-upgrades returns 200 for superadmin
Response has summary structure with critical, warning, total_needing_upgrade, potential_monthly_revenue
Response has admins list
Each admin entry has correct structure (admin_id, admin_name, admin_email, business_name, current_plan, recommended_plan, usage_alerts, urgency)
Usage alerts have correct structure (metric, current, limit, percentage, status)
Summary counts match admin list (total=4, critical=2, warning=2)
Potential monthly revenue is non-negative (₹3,996)
PUT /api/superadmin/admins/{id}/plan requires superadmin role
PUT /api/superadmin/admins/{id}/plan validates plan_id (rejects invalid plan)
PUT /api/superadmin/admins/{id}/plan returns 404 for unknown admin
Superadmin dashboard is accessible
Billing plans endpoint returns all 4 plans (free, basic, pro, enterprise)
UI: Upgrades tab visible with badge count (4)
UI: 4 summary cards displayed (Critical, Warning, Total, Revenue)
UI: Admin list shows CRITICAL and WARNING badges
UI: Progress bars show usage vs limits (e.g., Stores 4/1, Users 12/2)
UI: Upgrade button opens modal
UI: Modal shows admin info, current plan, recommended plan
UI: Modal has 3 plan options (Basic, Pro, Enterprise)
UI: Cancel button closes modal
UI: Overview tab has amber alert banner with View All button
critical_code_review_comments: Feature correctly identifies admins exceeding limits (critical) vs approaching 80% (warning)
Potential revenue calculation: sum of (recommended_plan_price - current_plan_price) for all admins
Plan change via PUT /api/superadmin/admins/{id}/plan creates new subscription and logs to superadmin_audit
Modal explicitly states 'without charging them' - for promotional/support upgrades only
seed_data_creation: No seed data created - used existing accounts
context_for_next_testing_agent: Super Admin Upgrades Tab fully implemented and tested. Navigation: Login as superadmin@bijnisbooks.com -> Super Admin Dashboard -> Click Upgrades tab (has badge with count). Current state: 4 admins needing upgrade (2 critical exceeding limits, 2 warning at 80%+). Potential monthly revenue: ₹3,996. Each admin card shows usage alerts with progress bars. Click Upgrade button to open plan selection modal with Basic/Pro/Enterprise options. Manual plan change does NOT charge the admin - meant for promotional/support cases. Overview tab shows amber alert banner linking to Upgrades tab.
